title: [Bug]: Priority metadata header accepts ASCII control characters
summary: The priority metadata header validator rejects newlines but still accepts other ASCII control characters, which is inconsistent with the stricter header validation used elsewhere in the project.
observed: A configuration can set message_metadata.priority.header to a value containing a control character and validation still succeeds.
expected: Metadata header names should fail closed when any ASCII control character is present.
reproduction: Construct MessageMetadataFieldConfig with a priority header containing a NUL or tab character and observe that validation accepts the value.
failing_test: Add a unit test that creates MessageMetadataFieldConfig(header='Priority\u0000Header') and expects Pydantic validation to fail.
impact: Unsafe header names can make runtime extraction ambiguous and weaken operator confidence in configuration validation.
delivery_semantics: Commit-then-ACK behavior is not changed. This is a configuration validation hardening fix before messages are processed.
security: The fix must reject hostile control characters without logging sensitive configuration values.
acceptance: Priority metadata header validation rejects all ASCII control characters.
The regression test fails before the fix and passes after the fix.
The validation message is understandable for operators.
No NATS ACK behavior, sink contract, or payload persistence semantics change.
Release notes mention the metadata validation hardening.
tests: Run the focused metadata boundary tests and the full local validation script.
documentation: Update release notes and test evidence. No public configuration syntax changes are required beyond stricter invalid input rejection.
closeout: Mark completed after the fix is merged into the release development branch with sanitized test evidence.
